disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The lump is hard, bony and pointed, sits exactly in the midline just below the breastbone, and does not move, reduce or change when pressed
trigger_question_examples: Can you describe exactly where the lump is and what it feels like?
Does the lump move or change when you press it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's rock hard and pointed, right in the very middle just under my breastbone. It stays exactly where it is when I press it — it doesn't squish or move.

trigger_question_examples: Have you lost any weight without trying recently?
Has your appetite or clothing size changed over the last few months?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, my weight has been completely stable, nothing like that.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No vomiting of blood and no black tarry stools
trigger_question_examples: Have you vomited any blood, or noticed black or tarry bowel motions?
Any signs of bleeding from your stomach or bowels?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, nothing like that at all — no blood, and my bowel movements are normal.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The area is completely painless and there is no abdominal pain
trigger_question_examples: Is the lump or your tummy sore in any way?
Do you have any abdominal pain at all?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, it doesn't hurt at all — that's part of what scares me, that something this big doesn't hurt.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The racing heart and fast breathing came on together with the fear of the lump; there is no chest pain, no pain radiating to the arm or jaw, and no relation to exertion
trigger_question_examples: Did the racing heart start with the fear, and do you have any chest pain or pain spreading to your arm or jaw?
Is the pounding heart worse with activity, or only since you got frightened?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The pounding heart and fast breathing only started once I found the lump and freaked out. No chest pain, nothing going into my arm or jaw, and it's not from moving around.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No personal history of cancer and previous check-ups have been normal
trigger_question_examples: Have you ever been diagnosed with cancer, or had any abnormal scans or blood tests before?
Have your previous health check-ups all been normal?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I've never had cancer and my past check-ups have all been fine.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The lump was first noticed while lying flat, and the patient has been searching the internet which escalated the fear
trigger_question_examples: What were you doing when you first noticed it, and what happened after?
Have you been looking things up online about it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I was lying flat in bed and felt it stick out, then I started Googling and completely spiralled into panic.
